transcript.text:
[Not your everyday jjajang tteokboki] (Rice cake with black bean sauce)
[This one comes with history and experience]
[Other dishes to go with it]
It's perfect
The soup is getting better and better
[What's going on here?]
[A peaceful tteokboki restaurant in Shindang-dong]
Hello everyone
I'm at Shindang-dong today
There's a place here known for their jjajang tteokboki (rice cake with black bean sauce)
50 years of tradition
Since year 1972 For 50 years!
I'm here because people are saying this place is great
[Jinmi Tteokboki] [Seoul, Shindang-dong]
I can smell the jjajang already
Hello

I'm inside now
[The menu is simple]
Small and simple
Is it 1 type of jjajang tteokboki?
Let's order everything
5 orders of tteokboki,
1 of everything else, please
Thank you
This amount of food is not for 1 person
Maybe I should've ordered just 3 Because there's no space
Would you like 1?
Enjoy
Everything is out
It smells like soybean paste A mix
Bon appetit
Which one first?

Tteokboki
About 3 pieces Flour rice cake
Am I right this time? I got it wrong before
It's made with rice
Is it?
Delicious
Really good
It's really...
I didn't expect this It's so good
I thought it'd taste like regular jjajang with rice cake
I love the texture of the rice cake they used here
It's not just jjajang sauce either It's like spicy rice cake
I can taste a lot more flavors in here than just jjajang
A regular spicy rice cake dish with a strong taste of jjajang?
The rice cake is excellent
I read on the blog that the fish cake is their signature

Let's give this a try then
Tasty!
Anyone would enjoy this It has a kick to it
This is just what I like
This is the best jjajang tteokboki I've ever had
Although I didn't try it that much before
-It's made with rice -Is it?
I got it right
Tasty
I love the rice cake It's perfect
It's chewy and sticky
Perfect consistency
Fish cake
The fish cake is chewy as well Doesn't taste like flour
It's very soft
You guys know one of my favorite tteokboki restaurant Jaeil Bunsik?

It's like that but in Jjajang
Tastes different but it's similar
Maybe because of the rice cake
I love it
Let's try the rest
Kimbap
Look at the glossiness
The ingredients It's packed
First time dipping kimbap in jjajang
Savory
So good
Kimbap needs to be nutty
Just like this one
Seasoned really well with sesame oil
Crispy
I didn't think it would be from the looks of it

It's a mini rice bowl
For water
Water here is great too
Soondae next (pork blood sausage)
You can't go wrong with this
1 more kimbap
This is really good It's nothing special
But it's seasoned so well
Calamari
I love it
Calamari and then fried seaweed roll
The squid is very soft
I didn't expect much But it's really good
It almost came out
Let's finish 1
They sell them not cooked too

It should be good if I take it home and cook it with noodles
Udon noodles? Chewy noodles? All should be good
Personally, ramyun noodles
I love this seat
Pork liver
It's a little spicy but it doesn't stack
A little bit I think
Yaki mandu (fried dumplings) It's been a while
It's supposed to go in here
It would be good soaked in jjajang sauce
I got fresh fishcake broth
Lovely
They use the same dishes too as Jaeil Bunsik
Jaeil Bunsik broth is more bold
A little bland here but still good
The soup is good

Wrap the rice cake with fishcake
It's really big
Let's put this here because the broth tastes good
It's a little spicy
Some soondae to cool down
This has sesame oil too So does this one
Greasiness kills spiciness
It's clean so far
Jjajang shows right away
-How long has it been here? -50 years
-My mother... -Your mother ran this place?
1972 Jinmi Tteokboki
Is Mabokrim the oldest tteokboki place?
As far as I know, yes From around here
This place is the 2nd oldest then?
I think so But I'm not too sure

You're so good at eating
I'm a big eater
I've never seen anything like it Ladies can eat up to 2 orders, even for big eaters
3 servings for grown men too
I wonder how much it was 50 years ago
1 piece for 10 Won
10 pieces for 100 Won (~$0.07 USD)
We would eat 1 or 2 pieces only because the rice cake pieces were big
Our allowance was 10 Won
We couldn't buy more than 10 pieces
So people would dine in and eat 2~3 pieces and leave?
Yes, they would pay 10 Won just for 1 piece to get a bite and leave
Rice cake was expensive back then
I see
I was born at the right time
Thank you

Rice cake without fishcake broth is like
having kimchi fried rice without kimchi
Eat something, drink as a palate cleanser It's great
This is getting better and better
Is it getting thicker?
It tastes great
Another one
I can move these in here
It hurts so much
I bit myself so hard
Will hot broth make it worse?
Not really
I've always had kimbap with regular tteokboki sauce
It should be good with fishcake broth, no?
It's good
Yaki mandu soaked in sauce

1 last bite
I don't have much experience with Jjajang Tteokboki,
But It was the best
Thanks everyone
She really finished everything
She looks familiar
She's just a good eater, right?
[Thank you for the meal] [Subtitles by Anthony Hyun Choi] [antchoi91@gmail.com]
